description: Tricks whose execution carries a spinning rotational modifier (full-body twist around vertical axis). Observational classification by spinning-token presence in notation OR canonical name.
Tricks carrying a paradox modifier (PDX flag in operational notation OR paradox token in canonical name). Inward-then-outward dex pair body mechanic.
Tricks carrying a pixie modifier (+1 universal set modifier per modifier table). Compact dex variant.
sample_members: dimwalk, smoke, phoenix, paste, pigbeater, smear, magellan, parkwalk

description: Tricks carrying a ducking body modifier (duck body action between dex steps).
Tricks carrying a diving body modifier (forward-downward dive action).
sample_members: Hatchet, Red Ribbon, Zulu Le Loup, Arachnophobia

description: Tricks carrying a symposium modifier (no-plant-while body frame held open across the trick).
Tricks carrying a stepping body modifier (step action interleaved between dexes).
Tricks carrying a tapping modifier (foot-pattern variant; distinct from stepping per pt3).
Tricks carrying an atomic modifier (set primitive; +1 non-rotational / +2 rotational).
Tricks carrying a furious modifier (set primitive parallel to atomic).
Tricks carrying a quantum modifier (+1 universal set modifier per pt10).
Tricks carrying a nuclear modifier (+2 set modifier; structurally paradox+atomic per pt10).
Tricks carrying a gyro modifier (rotation modifier; body-axial rotation).
Tricks carrying a whirling modifier (rotation modifier; +1 flat per pt10).
Tricks carrying a barraging modifier (+1 universal body modifier).
Tricks whose component-flag stack contains XDEX (full-circle dex variant). pt1 ruled XDEX narrow (specific tricks only).
Tricks whose canonical name contains 'walk' AND whose base or composition reads as a butterfly-wing variant. Crosses the modifier axis (stepping/pixie/blurry all produce -walk variants).
sample_members: ripwalk, sidewalk, bigwalk, dimwalk, parkwalk, arcwalk, tripwalk

description: Tricks with exactly one DEX flag in their operational notation stack; no body action between set and recovery.
Tricks with exactly two DEX flags; no body action between dexes.
Tricks with three-or-more DEX flags; no body action (rapid sequential dex chain).
Tricks whose final contact lands on a non-standard surface (UNS flag in operational notation; flapper / sole / unusual delay).
Tricks whose primary uptime dex executes inside-to-outside via the hip-driven motion (mirage canonical pattern).
sample_members: mirage, blur (Stepping Paradox Mirage), atom-smasher, sumo, smear
approx_member_count: many (anchor row mirage; modifier compounds inherit)

description: Tricks whose primary uptime dex executes outside-to-inside via the hip-driven motion (illusion canonical pattern).
approx_member_count: fewer than hippy-in (illusion family less central)

description: Tricks whose uptime mechanic is a leg-pass over the bag without completing a full circle (legover canonical pattern).
Tricks whose uptime mechanic is a full leg-circle around the bag (ATW canonical pattern).
Tricks whose uptime mechanic is the under-foot scoop motion (pickup canonical pattern).
Tricks whose uptime dex follows the butterfly wing-motion pattern (op-out dex with cross-body recovery).
sample_members: butterfly, ripwalk, sidewalk, bigwalk, dimwalk, parkwalk, dada-curve, matador

description: Tricks whose operational notation opens with Toe as the first contact (set on a toe-stall).
Tricks whose operational notation opens with Clip as the first contact.
Tricks whose operational notation opens with Set (generic set surface; bracket-style notation primarily).
Tricks whose final contact lands on a toe (Op Toe or Same Toe with DEL flag).
Tricks whose final contact lands on a clipper (Op Clip or Same Clip with XBD+DEL).
Tricks whose final contact lands on a flapper surface (UNS class).
Tricks whose operational notation begins with another canonical trick name as the substructure (substructure-as-set).
approx_member_count: 2 known patterns (Dragon 8x, Frigidosis 3x in FM corpus)

description: Tricks whose execution holds same-side body direction throughout (ss qualifier in records sort_name; ss=+0 per pt12).
sample_members: sidewalk, parkwalk, ripstein (double swirl ss), dada-curve
approx_member_count: many (54-row same-side display-suffix cohort in records)

description: Tricks whose final contact carries the XBD (cross-body) component flag (recovery surface on the opposite side from the plant foot).
sample_members: butterfly, ripwalk, sidewalk, dimwalk, parkwalk, phoenix

description: Tricks whose notation contains the (no plant while) pre-state flag (symposium-style; support leg held off-ground during specified beat).
sample_members: Alex Zerbe is the greatest, montage, superfly

description: Tricks whose notation contains the (rooted) pre-state flag (held-position; no support-leg plant per pt8).
sample_members: Railwalk, +2 other rows in FM corpus

description: Tricks marked as reverse-direction variants of an anchor row (direction-is-structural rule).
Tricks marked as 'double X' or 'triple X' that have been community-stabilized as canonical rows per pt8 (multiplicity policy §10).
Tricks whose asserted ADD does not reduce cleanly to a modifier+base arithmetic; per CANONICALIZATION_POLICY §3.2 stated-ADD-without-stated-structure class.
